subject: An overhead crime-scene tableau: a body lies sprawled on a richly patterned red carpet, viewed from above, with a kneeling investigator examining the scene. Splatters of blood pool around the figure, and a clue or document lies near the victim.
description: A top-down noir crime-scene illustration framed by the Murders at Karlov Manor showcase border, which is styled like a vintage detective pulp novel with cream margins and red corner flourishes. The central image looks down on a victim lying motionless on an ornate crimson rug, limbs awkwardly splayed. Dark blood pools and arcs across the rug's pattern. A second figure crouches near the body, perhaps an investigator gathering evidence. The palette is dominated by deep reds, blacks, and warm shadow tones, evoking a Victorian parlor murder. Flavor text references Teyva and Kaya, framing this as part of the Ravnica murder-mystery arc.
foreground: victim's body sprawled on patterned red carpet with blood spatter and a clue/document
background: ornate carpet pattern filling the floor of an interior room
emotion: still, grim, the heavy quiet of aftermath
